prompt: Write the opening paragraph of a children's book about a shy octopus who wants to make friends.
response: Deep down in a quiet cove, where the water sparkled like scattered sapphires and the sea fans swayed to secret songs, there lived a little octopus named Oliver. Now, Oliver had eight wiggly arms, two big curious eyes, and one very shy heart. Whenever another sea creature swam by, he would turn the exact color of the rock he was sitting on and hold his breath until they passed. But every night, when the moon painted silver ribbons across the ocean floor, Oliver would peek out from his cozy cave and whisper the same tiny wish to the bubbles floating up, up, up: *"Tomorrow, maybe tomorrow, I'll be brave enough to say hello."*
